rulings.Case Title: CRYSTAL LEI ET AL VS. DEMAS W YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, June 12, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order. Defendant Demas Yan's motion to set aside the orders entered on April 28, 2015 and June 6, 2015 is denied. At the time both orders were entered the court had personal jurisdiction over the parties and subject matter jurisdiction over the case. The Nutrition Distribution case cited by Mr. Yan did not in any way call into question the court's jurisdiction to make the two orders which Mr. Yan seeks to have set aside. Since Mr. Yan's motion is denied based on its evident lack of merit from reading the moving papers, there is no need for the court to address the request by plaintiffs Crystal Lei and Bryant Fu to require Mr. Yan to post security, which was made in opposition to this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Plaintiffs are required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MEGAN KOSNOSKI VS. NO MORE DIRT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 12, 2018, Line 3, CROSS COMPLAINANT FAIRMONT HOTEL MANAGEMENT, L.P. Motion For Protective Order And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Stephanie Krmpotic, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The matter is continued to June 20, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to JSKrmpotic@LOWBALL.COM with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HK)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA COHEN VS. UNUM GROUP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Written Responses To Her Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, Against Defendants Unum Group And Provident Life & Accident Insurance Company
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 12, 2018, Line 2, Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Written Responses To Her Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, Against Defendants Unum Group And Provident Life & Accident Insurance Company. Pro Tem Judge Stephanie Krmpotic, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel is granted in part and denied in part as follows: As to Request No.'s 32, 33, 39-40, 42-45, 47 Defendant are ordered to provide verified code compliant supplemental responses, stating that after a diligent search and reasonable inquiry, it will produce all responsive documents in its possession, custody or control; and produce responsive documents within 20-days of this order. If Defendant's withhold any documents on the basis of privilege, those documents must be identified in a privilege log. If redaction of private information and claims numbers becomes unduly burdensome, the parties shall meet and confer regarding this issue. The parties are ordered to meet and confer and plaintiff is to submit revised requests within 10-days regarding the following Requests: Request Nos. 30-31 are to be narrowed as to time; Request Nos. 34, 35, 36, 37, 38, 39, 46, are overbroad and should be more narrowly tailored to specify the specific documents to be produced related to disability claims and recovery; Request Nos. 48, 49, and 50 are to be narrowed as to time and a more narrow scope of the documents to be produced. Defendants shall have 20-days thereafter to serve verified supplemental responses and documents to the revised requests, subject to a privilege log for documents withheld based on privilege. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to JSKrmpotic@LOWBALL.COM with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HK)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK FLOYD VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, June 12, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Ford Motor Company's demurrer to the fifth and seventh causes of action in the first amended complaint plaintiff filed by plaintiff Patrick Floyd is sustained without leave to amend as to fifth cause of action for breach of the implied warranty of merchantability and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to seventh cause of action for fraud by omission. The implied warranty claim is barred by the four year statute of limitations. Commercial Code 2725(2) provides that: "A cause of action accrues when the breach occurs, regardless of the aggrieved party's lack of knowledge of the breach. A breach of warranty occurs when tender of delivery is made, except that where a warranty explicitly extends to future performance of the goods and discovery of the breach must await the time of such performance the cause of action accrues when the breach is or should have been discovered." Mr. Floyd alleges that he purchased the vehicle in September of 2012 and he filed his action in September 2017. For the statute of limitations for the implied warranty claim, the breach occurred when Mr. Floyd purchased the vehicle, i.e., when the tender of delivery happened. The accrual exception based on discovery does not apply to the implied warranty claim. The future performance exception "applies only when the seller has expressly agreed to warrant its product for a specific and defined period of time." (Cardinal Health 301, Inc. v. Tyco Electronics Corp. (2008) 169 Cal.App.4th 116, 130 (emphasis in original)). The Cardinal Health court explained that "[b]ecause an implied warranty is one that arises by operation of law rather than by an express agreement of the parties, courts have consistently held [that] it is not a warranty that explicitly extends to future performance of the goods." (Id. at 134.) Mr. Floyd is given leave to amend his fraud claim to allege with more particularity the delayed discovery of the alleged fraud. (Hobart v. Hobart Estate Co. (1945) 26 Cal.2d 412, 437; Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808.) Ford's other arguments are rejected. While Bigler-Engler v. Breg, Inc. (2017) 7 Cal.App.5th 276, 310-311 supports Ford's position that Mr. Floyd does not plead a transaction to support the concealment claim, the weight of authority indicates that such a transaction is not necessary under the instant circumstances. (Jones v. Conocophillips (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 1187, 1199; OCM Principal Opportunities Fund v. CIBC World Markets Corp. (2007) 157 Cal.App.4th 836, 859; Reniger v. Hyandai Motor America (2015) 122 F.Supp.3d 888, 896.) Paragraphs 47 and 50 of the amended complaint sufficiently allege Ford's "exclusive knowledge" to support the concealment claim. If Mr. Floyd is able to allege a timely concealment claim, the economic loss rule is inapplicable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ford is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO SANTA CLARA ** TONI YOUNG VS. MICHAEL E. ADAMS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, June 12, 2018, Line 10, DEFENDANT MICHAEL ADAMS' Notice Of Motion To Change Venue. Defendant Michael Adams' motion to change venue to Santa Clara County is granted. Mr. Adams' motion is not untimely since it was filed within the time he was granted to respond to the complaint. The contract venue provision providing for venue in the county where the obligation was to be performed in inapplicable because that provision is only applicable where there is a writing providing where the contract is to be performed and may not be shown by parol evidence. (Weil and Brown book at 3:503-504). Plaintiff Toni Young must pay all transfer costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Adams is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SEVGI CURTIS ET AL VS. PG&E CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, June 12, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANT PG&E CORPORATION AND PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants PG&E Corporation and Pacific Gas & Electric Company's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Segvi Curtis and Janos Libor is granted without leave to amend but without prejudice to plaintiffs' filing a motion for leave to add punitive damages allegations if plaintiffs later obtain information that defendants' employees and defendants acted in a way that supports punitive damages liability against either or both of the defendants. The complaint does not allege facts showing either malice, fraud or oppression by defendants' employees or conduct by defendants that supports imposition of punitive damages liability on a corporate employer. Nor does it appear that, at this stage of the case, there is any basis to believe that plaintiffs will be able to do so in good faith. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
